message:
chore(beads): update issue tracking state

Adds new dependency links and status transitions to .beads/issues.jsonl
reflecting recently completed work including the config merge fix,
package_manager refactoring, and cohere provider corrections. Three
new issue entries added for tracking ongoing work items.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add baseline fixtures that validate each extension shape in isolation.

# Deliverables
- Minimal fixtures for skills, prompts, tools, MCP servers, providers, templates.
- Expected behaviors: load, list capabilities, basic invocation.
- Deterministic outputs for conformance assertions.

# Notes
These fixtures ensure the harness works before scaling to real extensions.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:28:51.367391543Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T05:17:40.023660807Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T05:17:40.023569647Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-16i7","depends_on_id":"bd-2fps","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:14Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-16i7","depends_on_id":"bd-ljzb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:14Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":3761,"issue_id":"bd-16i7","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Background: We need minimal fixtures to validate each extension type before testing real artifacts.

Reasoning: Base fixtures provide deterministic smoke coverage and isolate harness bugs.

Considerations: Keep fixtures tiny and deterministic; avoid external dependencies.","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:51:53Z"},{"id":3762,"issue_id":"bd-16i7","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Completed: Base fixtures for all 8 extension shapes.

Fixtures (in tests/ext_conformance/artifacts/base_fixtures/):
- minimal_tool: registerTool('greet') with JSON Schema params and execute handler
- minimal_command: registerCommand('ping') returning 'pong'
- minimal_provider: registerProvider with models + streamSimple stub
- minimal_event: pi.on('agent_start') event hook
- minimal_ui_component: registerMessageRenderer for 'test/plain' content type
- minimal_configuration: registerFlag('verbose') + registerShortcut('ctrl+shift+v')
- minimal_multi: registerTool('echo') + pi.on('agent_start') (two distinct types)
- minimal_resources: General shape (export default, no registrations)

Fixture JSON (in tests/ext_conformance/fixtures/):
- Added minimal_configuration.json, minimal_ui_component.json, minimal_multi.json
- Each has scenarios with appropriate expectations

Shape tests (tests/ext_conformance_shapes.rs):
- Added 3 new tests: configuration, ui_component, multi
- Extended batch from 5 to 8 shapes (all shapes covered)
- Results: 7/8 pass, 1 known gap (ui_component message_renderers not tracked in snapshot yet)
- 66 total tests pass

Fixed fixture bug: minimal_configuration used wrong registerFlag API (single object vs name+spec).","created_at":"2026-02-07T05:17:34Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-16kl","title":"Unit: session persistence corruption + edge cases (no mocks)","description":"# Goal
Cover session persistence edge cases in `src/session.rs` without mocks, focusing on corruption recovery and branching semantics.

# Why / User Impact
Session files are the backbone of continuity. Corruption recovery, atomic writes, and branch metadata must be robust so users never lose work.

# Scope (Granular)
- Corrupted JSONL lines: verify they are skipped, warnings emitted, and remaining entries load.
- Leaf selection: ensure `leaf_id` points to last valid entry after corruption.
- Branch summaries + compaction summaries: verify serialization, render paths, and round‑trip invariants.
- Session save: verify atomic write behavior and deterministic naming under temp dirs.
- Export path handling: invalid path / permission denied surfaces helpful errors.

# Logging / Artifacts
- Use TestHarness/TestLogger; capture temp file paths and stderr warnings.
- Record corrupted JSONL fixtures and any exported files as artifacts.

# Acceptance Criteria
- Tests in `tests/session_conformance.rs` or a new `tests/session_persistence.rs`.
- No mocks; real filesystem temp dirs only.
- Deterministic, race‑free assertions (avoid wall‑clock coupling).
- Explicit assertions on warning text and recovered message count.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead (add regression coverage if applicable)
[ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale in notes) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
[ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Any new fixtures/golden outputs are deterministic and documented
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T04:58:04.314887696Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T19:25:00.493559771Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T08:49:02.096447508Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-16kl","depends_on_id":"bd-102","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:07Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-16uv","title":"Perf: eliminate per-chunk allocations in SSE stream (SseStream)","description":"# Goal
Reduce CPU + allocation churn on provider streaming path by removing per-chunk String/Vec allocations in src/sse.rs (SseStream UTF-8 handling).

# Why
SSE streaming is on the hot path for all providers. Today SseStream copies bytes into utf8_buffer, then allocates an owned String for valid UTF-8, then copies again into SseParser\x27s buffer. It also allocates a new Vec for trailing bytes when UTF-8 is split across chunks.

# Approach (Extreme Optimization)
- Baseline: add a micro-bench covering SseStream parsing with realistic chunk sizes.
- Optimize: feed &str slices directly from utf8_buffer into SseParser (no intermediate String) and keep trailing bytes in-place (no to_vec).
- Prove: existing SSE unit tests + added chunked UTF-8 regression coverage.

# Acceptance
- [ ] No behavior change (all SSE tests pass)
- [ ] Micro-bench shows improved throughput for chunked inputs
- [ ] Quality gates pass","notes":"## Implementation
- `src/sse.rs`: add a fast-path in `SseStream::poll_next_event` to avoid copying the full incoming chunk into `utf8_buffer` when there is no pending UTF-8 tail.
- Keeps existing behavior for invalid UTF-8 (surface `InvalidData`) and for partial UTF-8 sequences (buffer remainder, continue parsing next chunk).

## Measurement (Criterion)
Command:
- `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/pi_agent_rust_target_blackfalcon cargo bench --bench tools sse_stream -- --noplot`

Before:
- `sse_stream/parse/64`:   `[883.52 us 889.96 us 895.67 us]`
- `sse_stream/parse/1024`: `[688.67 us 693.27 us 698.09 us]`
- `sse_stream/parse/4096`: `[692.80 us 698.86 us 704.64 us]`

After:
- `sse_stream/parse/64`:   `[841.65 us 850.27 us 858.67 us]` (mean ~-4.6% time; p<0.05)
- `sse_stream/parse/1024`: `[676.31 us 689.18 us 700.43 us]` (within noise threshold)
- `sse_stream/parse/4096`: `[682.95 us 688.83 us 698.19 us]` (no change detected)

## Isomorphism Proof
- Ordering preserved: underlying byte stream order is unchanged; only an intermediate copy is removed.
- Delimiters preserved: UTF-8 validation still gates feeding; the same bytes are fed into `SseParser` in the same sequence.
- Error behavior preserved: invalid UTF-8 still returns `InvalidData`.

## Gates
- `cargo fmt --check` OK
- `cargo check --all-targets` OK
- `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` OK
- `cargo test` OK","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BlackFalcon","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:07:46.482214999Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T11:01:42.589166154Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T11:01:42.589138733Z","close_reason":"Completed (SseStream fast-path + bench improvement + gates green)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-16v","title":"Create Extension Conformance Report (evidence binder)","description":"Background:
- We need a single place that maps extension -> evidence.

Steps:
- Generate a table of extensions with version, runtime tier, features tested, and pass/fail.
- Link to fixture files and harness output logs.
- Include summary stats (coverage %, failures, gaps).

Acceptance:
- Report is self-contained and can be regenerated.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss

 {"id":"bd-1wtr","title":"Interactive editor: wire editor_padding_x + autocomplete_max_visible + show_hardware_cursor","description":"# Goal
Wire editor-related settings from `src/config.rs` into interactive rendering.

# Settings
- `editor_padding_x`: horizontal padding for the editor (0-3).
- `autocomplete_max_visible`: max visible items in autocomplete dropdown.
- `show_hardware_cursor`: show terminal cursor (legacy: off by default).

# Current Gap
These config fields exist but are unused.

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Editor padding affects input rendering.
- [ ] Autocomplete respects max visible setting.
- [ ] Hardware cursor is toggled appropriately via terminal control.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss

Use artifact manifests and version pins from acquisition tasks.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:29:02.172962809Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T05:56:59.345746902Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T05:56:59.345655752Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1x31","depends_on_id":"bd-16i7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:09Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1x31","depends_on_id":"bd-2fps","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:09Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1x31","depends_on_id":"bd-3uvd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:09Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1x31","depends_on_id":"bd-3vb8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:09Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":3381,"issue_id":"bd-1x31","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Background: The project goal is to prove real extensions work unmodified, not just synthetic fixtures.

Reasoning: Per‑extension cases demonstrate compatibility at scale and catch real-world edge cases.

Considerations: Keep outputs deterministic and include clear error diagnostics for failures.","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:52:04Z"},{"id":3382,"issue_id":"bd-1x31","author":"LavenderRobin","text":"QUALITY GATES: SCALE + DIAGNOSTICS

For a 200+ extension Tier-1 corpus, per-extension cases must be cheap to run and easy to debug.

Requirements
- Each extension case must:
  - state the scenario(s) exercised
  - assert key outputs + side effects
  - record timings (load + exec)

Logging/artifacts
- On failure, save:
  - normalized output
  - diff vs expected
  - harness JSONL log excerpt for that extension

Determinism
- All cases must run in offline/fixture mode in CI (no live network).

Maintenance
- Add a “case generator” or template so adding new extensions is fast and consistent.","created_at":"2026-02-05T08:15:45Z"},{"id":3383,"issue_id":"bd-1x31","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Claiming. All blockers closed. Will create per-extension conformance cases with coverage tracking, using the existing harness infrastructure.","created_at":"2026-02-07T05:46:59Z"},{"id":3384,"issue_id":"bd-1x31","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Completed: All 223 extensions tested against conformance matrix. Results: 187/223 pass (83.9%). Failure classification: 22 manifest_registration_mismatch, 5 missing_npm_package, 4 multi_file_dependency, 4 runtime_error, 1 test_fixture. Stored baseline in conformance_baseline.json, updated CONFORMANCE_REPORT.md and conformance_summary.json. Coverage: 100% of manifest exercised.","created_at":"2026-02-07T05:56:53Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1x7y","title":"Conformance: Tier 2c — Tool+Event Combo Extensions (3 exts)","description":"Conformance tests for extensions registering both tools AND event hooks with stateful behavior. Extensions (3): 1. todo.ts — todo tool + /todos command + session_start event to reconstruct state from tool results 2. notify.ts — Desktop notifications via OSC 777 + event hooks 3. antigravity-image-gen.ts — Image generation tool using HTTP with OAuth flow. Test full lifecycle: registration, first invocation, state persistence across events, subsequent invocations. Use HTTP mocks for antigravity. Test error paths (missing credentials, network failure).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T06:17:04.120761447Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T01:31:56.723519787Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T01:31:56.723380528Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1x7y","depends_on_id":"bd-1y3m","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:14Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1xbr","title":"Fresh-eyes random-core bug audit and fixes","description":"Randomly sample core Rust modules, trace execution/import flows, identify obvious bugs or fragile logic with first-principles review, and land high-confidence fixes with full Rust quality gates.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-07T20:49:51.112161291Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T21:31:26.026611216Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T21:31:26.026518423Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":2903,"issue_id":"bd-1xbr","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Fixed finalize_assistant_message() in agent.rs: added defensive type check. When added_partial=true but last message isn't Assistant (edge case), code now pushes a new message and emits MessageStart instead of silently overwriting an unrelated message. Also audited tools.rs, session.rs, sse.rs, auth.rs, compaction.rs - no other high-confidence bugs found.","created_at":"2026-02-07T21:18:41Z"},{"id":2904,"issue_id":"bd-1xbr","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Fresh-eyes bug audit complete. 6 fixes applied across 5 files:

**Fix 1 - SSE UTF-8 error path (sse.rs)**: Valid UTF-8 prefix before invalid byte was silently dropped. Both fast and slow paths now feed valid prefix to parser before returning error.

**Fix 2 - Stream end stale partial (agent.rs)**: Stream ending without Done event left stale incomplete AssistantMessage in messages vec. Now pops stale partial before returning error.

**Fix 3 - update_partial_message type guard (agent.rs)**: update_partial_message blindly overwrote last message regardless of type. Added Message::Assistant(_) pattern guard.

**Fix 4 - File permission preservation (tools.rs)**: Edit and write tools used tempfile::persist() which defaults to 0o600 permissions, destroying original permissions (e.g. executable scripts). Now captures and restores original permissions.

**Fix 5 - Anthropic signature_delta handling (anthropic.rs)**: signature_delta events for thinking blocks were silently dropped. Added signature field to AnthropicDelta and handler that stores thinking_signature on ThinkingContent. This is required for multi-turn extended thinking conversations.

**Fix 6 - Provider silent stream termination (anthropic.rs, openai.rs)**: Both providers returned None when SSE stream ended before message_stop/[DONE], causing silent loss of accumulated partial message. Now emit StreamEvent::Done with partial. Added done flag to prevent double-emission.

All tests pass: cargo check, clippy -D warnings, cargo fmt, 11 Anthropic + 7 OpenAI provider tests.","created_at":"2026-02-07T21:31:12Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-jt3k","title":"Task: Wire Hostcall Dispatcher into Agent Loop","description":"# Task: Wire Hostcall Dispatcher into Agent Loop

## Objective

Integrate the ExtensionDispatcher into src/agent.rs so that hostcalls are processed during the agent's execution cycle. THIS IS THE CRITICAL INTEGRATION POINT.

## Background

The agent loop (src/agent.rs) orchestrates:
1. Building context (system prompt, messages, tools)
2. Calling provider.stream() to get model response
3. Processing stream events (text, thinking, tool calls)
4. Executing tools and appending results
5. Iterating until done

The extension runtime runs alongside this but is currently disconnected. We need to:
1. Initialize the extension runtime on agent start
2. Process pending hostcalls after each JS tick
3. Handle async hostcall execution without blocking streaming
4. Clean up on agent shutdown

## Current Agent Structure (src/agent.rs)

```rust
impl Agent {
    pub async fn run(
        &self,
        initial_message: Option<&str>,
        on_event: impl Fn(AgentEvent),
    ) -> Result<Vec<Message>> {
        // ... message handling loop
        loop {
            // Build context
            // Call provider.stream()
            // Process events
            // Execute tools
            // [MISSING: Process extension hostcalls]
        }
    }
}
```

## Implementation Plan

### 1. Add ExtensionDispatcher to Agent

```rust
pub struct Agent {
    provider: Arc<dyn Provider>,
    tools: Arc<ToolRegistry>,
    config: AgentConfig,
    // NEW: Extension runtime and dispatcher
    extension_runtime: Option<Arc<PiJsRuntime>>,
    extension_dispatcher: Option<Arc<ExtensionDispatcher>>,
}
```

### 2. Initialize Extensions on Agent Creation

```rust
impl Agent {
    pub async fn new_with_extensions(
        provider: Arc<dyn Provider>,
        tools: Arc<ToolRegistry>,
        config: AgentConfig,
        extension_paths: Vec<PathBuf>,
        session: Arc<dyn ExtensionSession>,
        ui_handler: Arc<dyn ExtensionUiHandler>,
    ) -> Result<Self> {
        // Load extensions
        let runtime = Arc::new(PiJsRuntime::new().await?);
        
        for path in &extension_paths {
            runtime.load_extension(path).await?;
        }
        
        // Create dispatcher
        let dispatcher = Arc::new(ExtensionDispatcher::new(
            runtime.clone(),
            tools.clone(),
            HttpConnector::new(),
            session,
            ui_handler,
            config.cwd.clone(),
        ));
        
        Ok(Self {
            provider,
            tools,
            config,
            extension_runtime: Some(runtime),
            extension_dispatcher: Some(dispatcher),
        })
    }
}
```

### 3. Process Hostcalls in Agent Loop

```rust
impl Agent {
    async fn process_extension_hostcalls(&self) -> Result<()> {
        if let Some(dispatcher) = &self.extension_dispatcher {
            dispatcher.process_pending().await?;
        }
        Ok(())
    }
    
    pub async fn run(...) -> Result<Vec<Message>> {
        loop {
            // ... existing loop body ...
            
            // Process any pending extension hostcalls
            self.process_extension_hostcalls().await?;
            
            // ... continue loop ...
        }
    }
}
```

### 4. Call Points for Hostcall Processing

Hostcalls should be processed at these points:
1. After extension initialization (activate() may make hostcalls)
2. After each tool execution (tools may trigger extension hooks)
3. After processing user input (input hooks may make hostcalls)
4. During idle periods (timer-based hostcalls)

## Async Execution Concerns

**Problem**: Hostcalls may be async (HTTP requests, tool execution) and shouldn't block streaming.

**Solution**: Use asupersync's structured concurrency:

```rust
async fn process_pending(&self) -> Result<()> {
    let requests = self.runtime.drain_hostcall_requests();
    
    // Spawn each hostcall as a concurrent task
    let handles: Vec<_> = requests.into_iter().map(|req| {
        let dispatcher = self.clone();
        asupersync::spawn(async move {
            let outcome = dispatcher.dispatch(req.kind).await;
            (req.call_id, outcome)
        })
    }).collect();
    
    // Wait for all and complete
    for handle in handles {
        let (call_id, outcome) = handle.await;
        self.runtime.complete_hostcall(&call_id, outcome)?;
    }
    
    Ok(())
}
```

## Error Handling

- Hostcall errors should NOT crash the agent
- Errors return as HostcallOutcome::Error
- JS Promise rejects, extension handles error
- Log errors for debugging

## Testing Strategy

1. Unit test: Agent with extensions initializes correctly
2. Unit test: Hostcalls processed after tool execution
3. Integration test: Extension tool call works end-to-end
4. Integration test: Extension HTTP call works
5. Stress test: Many concurrent hostcalls

## Dependencies

- Depends on: bd-389q (ExtensionDispatcher struct)
- Depends on: All hostcall handler tasks
- Blocks: End-to-end extension functionality

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Agent can be created with extension support
- [ ] Extensions loaded and activated on startup
- [ ] Hostcalls processed at appropriate points in loop
- [ ] Async hostcalls don't block streaming
- [ ] Errors handled gracefully
- [ ] Integration tests pass","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:55:16.137021531Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T00:24:16.386109037Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T00:24:16.386039217Z","close_reason":"Landed JS extension runtime wiring into AgentSession/main->interactive; added JS runtime pump_once + integration tests; gates green (dbc41cf)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-jt3k","depends_on_id":"bd-1hkm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:07Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-jt3k","depends_on_id":"bd-1tyz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:07Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-jt3k","depends_on_id":"bd-1u4t","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:07Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-jt3k","depends_on_id":"bd-2d99","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:07Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-jt3k","depends_on_id":"bd-2uhs","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:07Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-jt3k","depends_on_id":"bd-30j8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:07Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-jt3k","depends_on_id":"bd-37qz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:07Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-jt3k","depends_on_id":"bd-389q","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:07Z","created_by":"import"}]}
